title: phaseolus pubescens
intro: Phaseolus pubescens, also known as the Jacob's climbing bean or scarlet runner bean, is a perennial legume native to Central and South America. It has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and is believed to have various health benefits such as reducing blood sugar levels and improving cardiovascular health. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat conditions like digestive issues and fever. Additionally, the plant's leaves and flowers can be used in cooking and as a natural dye, while its seeds are a rich source of protein and can be consumed as a food source.
